Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Many SWE-bench-Passing PRs would not be merged 33 upvotes, 1 comments
  2. Physicist Astrid Eichhorn is a leader in the field of asymptotic safety 98 upvotes, 14 comments
  3. Fungal Electronics (2021) 50 upvotes, 6 comments
  4. Temporal: A nine-year journey to fix time in JavaScript 417 upvotes, 142 comments
  5. Don't post generated/AI-edited comments. HN is for conversation between humans. 1913 upvotes, 742 comments
  6. Show HN: I built a tool that watches webpages and exposes changes as RSS 118 upvotes, 35 comments
  7. Making WebAssembly a first-class language on the Web 323 upvotes, 124 comments
  8. Show HN: Open-source browser for AI agents 85 upvotes, 26 comments
  9. How we hacked McKinsey's AI platform 355 upvotes, 145 comments
  10. Building a TB-303 from Scratch 200 upvotes, 82 comments
  11. BitNet: 100B Param 1-Bit model for local CPUs 276 upvotes, 141 comments
  12. Show HN: Vanilla JavaScript refinery simulator built to explain job to my kids 75 upvotes, 37 comments
  13. Britain is ejecting hereditary nobles from Parliament after 700 years 51 upvotes, 23 comments
  14. Show HN: Satellite imagery object detection using text prompts 31 upvotes, 12 comments
  15. Can the Dictionary Keep Up? 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  16. The dead Internet is not a theory anymore 280 upvotes, 182 comments
  17. Launch HN: Sentrial (YC W26) – Catch AI agent failures before your users do 22 upvotes, 8 comments
  18. Show HN: Klaus – OpenClaw on a VM, batteries included 99 upvotes, 58 comments
  19. 5,200 holes carved into a Peruvian mountain left by an ancient economy 71 upvotes, 41 comments
  20. Entities enabling scientific fraud at scale (2025) 242 upvotes, 171 comments
  21. Google closes deal to acquire Wiz 182 upvotes, 125 comments
  22. Launch HN: Prism (YC X25) – Workspace and API to generate and edit videos 30 upvotes, 15 comments
  23. Lego's 0.002mm specification and its implications for manufacturing (2025) 326 upvotes, 283 comments
  24. Personal Computer by Perplexity 51 upvotes, 35 comments
  25. I'm glad the Anthropic fight is happening now 96 upvotes, 96 comments
  26. I was interviewed by an AI bot for a job 62 upvotes, 59 comments
  27. The MacBook Neo 307 upvotes, 522 comments
  28. What Is a Tort? 19 upvotes, 21 comments
  29. Swiss e-voting pilot can't count 2,048 ballots after decryption failure 123 upvotes, 291 comments
  30. Meticulous (YC S21) is hiring to redefine software dev 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 6:30am, 12 March 2026. Source here.
